senses.gloss: to cut into strips, to snithe into slices
to cut, to wound with a knife

examples.text: فَلَمَّا سَمِعَتْ بِمَكْرِهِنَّ أَرْسَلَتْ إِلَيْهِنَّ وَأَعْتَدَتْ لَهُنَّ مُتَّكَأً وَآتَتْ كُلَّ وَاحِدَةٍ مِّنْهُنَّ سِكِّينًا وَقَالَتِ ٱخْرُجْ عَلَيْهِنَّ ۖ فَلَمَّا رَأَيْنَهُ أَكْبَرْنَهُ وَقَطَّعْنَ أَيْدِيَهُنَّ وَقُلْنَ حَاشَ لِلَّهِ مَا هَٰذَا بَشَرًا إِنْ هَٰذَا إِلَّا مَلَكٌ كَرِيمٌ

senses.gloss: twig cut off at the end
a short but broad cusp of an arrow

examples.text:
وَلَيْلَةِ نَحْسٍ يَصْطَلِيَ القَوْسَ رَبُّهَا … وَأَقْطُعَهُ ٱللَّاتِي بِهَا يَتَنَبَّلُ
دَعَسْتُ عَلَى غَطَشٍ وَبَغْشٍ وَصُحْبَتِي … سُعَارٌ وَإِرْزِيزٌ وَوَجْرٌ وَأَفْكُلُ
examples.english:
And the ill-fated night when the master warmed up his bow, … and his arrowtips, in the handling of which he had trow
I trod upon darkness and rain-shower and my following … was the heat of night and snowy hail and timid gulps and shivering

senses.gloss: rug laid upon a camel the ends of which cover its shoulderblades
fief given by a prince to one of his servants for usufruct

etymology.text: Dictionnaire du faisceau dialectal arabe andalou reckons this verb a borrowing from Aramaic קְטַע / ܩܛܰܥ (qṭaʕ), while جَدَعَ (jadaʕa) would be the original. For related native terms see قَتَّ (qatta).
